name: Pat Toomey
summary: Former Pennsylvania Senator who supported Trump on most policies but became a vocal critic after the 2020 election, voting to convict Trump in his second impeachment trial and condemning his actions surrounding January 6.

keyStatements.quote: I do think the president committed impeachable offenses, but I don't know what's going to land on the Senate floor, if anything.
President Trump's conduct was unconscionable. He dishonored his office and betrayed the country.
keyStatements.context: Statement explaining his vote to convict Trump in second impeachment

keyStatements.quote: I don't think he should be the nominee... I don't think he can win. And I think if he's the nominee, we're very likely to lose the Senate and the House.

background: Pat Toomey served as U.S. Senator from Pennsylvania from 2011 to 2023. A fiscal conservative and former president of the Club for Growth, Toomey generally supported Trump's economic policies and judicial appointments. However, he became increasingly critical of Trump's behavior, particularly his false claims about the 2020 election and role in the January 6 Capitol attack. Toomey announced he would not seek re-election in 2022.
oppositionReasons: Condemned Trump's attempts to overturn 2020 election results
Believed Trump's actions surrounding January 6 were impeachable
Concerned Trump's 2024 candidacy would hurt Republican electoral prospects
Opposed Trump's false claims about election fraud
Viewed Trump's post-election conduct as betrayal of office
notableActions: One of seven Republican senators to vote to convict Trump in second impeachment trial
Called for Trump to resign after January 6 attack
Publicly stated Trump should not be 2024 Republican nominee
Defended 2020 election integrity against Trump's claims
Retired from Senate rather than face pro-Trump primary challenge

strengths: Two-term U.S. Senator from crucial swing state
Supported Trump on most policy matters, showing independence on criticism
Strong conservative credentials on fiscal and economic issues
Former president of influential Club for Growth
Reputation for principled stands on constitutional matters
considerations: Generally supportive of Trump policies until post-election period
Retired rather than facing potential pro-Trump primary opponent
Some conservatives viewed impeachment vote as betrayal
Limited criticism of Trump during most of his presidency
